name: Giani Darshan Singh Sohal (UK)
body: Giani Darshan Singh Sohal was a celebrated musician and one of the most distinguished names in the field of Gurbani Kirtan. He was born in 1933 at Hoshiarpur district of Punjab into a family that was deeply connected to classical music. Music was introduced into the family by his grandfather, Sant Karam Singh Ji. Giani Darshan Singh Ji’s father, Sant Surain Singh Ji, was a highly regarded scholar of the ‘Vedas’ and Gurbani. He carried on the tradition of music by learning Kirtan and Mridang from the legendary Bhai Samund Singh Ji, who served as Hazoori Raagi in Nankana Sahib.

Giani Darshan Singh Ji was introduced to music from a very young age by his uncle, Sardar Chanchal Singh, who was a student of the Lucknow School of Music. Next, he came under the guidance of renowned maestro of Gwalior Gharana of music, Giani Gian Singh Ji Almast. He trained under the watchful eye of Almast Ji for a period of 17 years, and during this time Almast Ji bestowed upon him many exquisite compositions. Almast Ji held Giani Darshan Singh Ji in very high esteem and referred to him as his favourite disciple.

Giani Darshan Singh Ji served the Sangat of United Kingdom (UK) with Kirtan for several decades, and he also taught Kirtan to many keen students spanning multiple generations. He had a vast repertoire of compositions consisting of many difficult Raags and Taals, which he sang regularly and delighted listeners. Giani Darshan Singh Ji completed his journey in this world and took his last breath in Punjab on 14 th November 2019.

Most of the information above was taken from Giani Darshan Singh Ji’s official website, which is not active anymore. A special thanks to ‘GyanKhand’ for saving the information about Giani Ji and providing it to us. If anyone has more recordings of Giani Darshan Singh Sohal, please contact us.
